reasoning:
As of the forecast date (October 26, 2025), Zohran Mamdani is the Democratic nominee for the NYC mayoral election and holds a commanding lead in all non-partisan polling. Recent polls from AARP/Gotham (Oct 20), Fox News (Oct 16), and Emerson (Sept 18) consistently place Mamdani in 1st place with 43-52% of the vote, while his nearest competitor, Independent Andrew Cuomo, trails by 14-24 points (typically polling in the high 20s). Republican Curtis Sliwa is in a distant 3rd place with 14-19%. 

For Mamdani to resolve YES for second place, he would need to be overtaken by another candidate (most likely Cuomo) while remaining ahead of all others. While the Cuomo campaign has claimed internal polls show a "dead heat," independent data does not support a late-stage collapse of Mamdani's lead in this heavily Democratic city. Given the 15-point average lead and the start of early voting showing strong turnout, the likelihood of Mamdani falling exactly into 2nd place is extremely low. It would require a massive and unprecedented polling error or a total consolidation of anti-Mamdani voters behind Cuomo that has not materialized in the data.
